description: The Fadaiyan-e Khalq (Majority) split from the [Organization of Iranian People's Fadai Guerrillas](/orgs/organization-of-iranian-peoples-fadai-guerrillas) in 1980, adopting a pro-Soviet line similar to the [Tudeh Party](/orgs/tudeh-party-of-iran). The Majority faction abandoned armed struggle and initially supported the Islamic Republic, believing in a two-stage revolution theory where the anti-imperialist phase must precede socialist transformation. This collaboration with the regime proved disastrous: when the government cracked down on leftists in 1983, many Majority members were arrested, imprisoned, and executed alongside Tudeh members in the [1988 mass executions](/events/mass-executions-1988). Surviving members fled to exile. The organization continues to operate from abroad, having evolved toward social-democratic positions.
editorial_notes: Pro-Soviet faction. Many members killed in 1988 mass executions. Now social-democratic in orientation.
